profile.source: Tutor-provided 15-lesson curriculum. Topics fixed by the tutor; islands, mechanics, difficulty and framing applied in Monkey Archipelago style.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will continue number and shape patterns, state the rule of a sequence, fill missing terms and spot the odd one out.
lessons.rationale: Patterns are one of Romy's strengths, so the plan opens here to build confidence before the harder fractions block. It also quietly rehearses skip-counting (2s, 5s, 10s), the backbone the child will lean on for fractions and times.
lessons.mechanic_rationale: pattern_input and drag_sort make the sequence tangible; find_unknown and fill_blank push the child to reverse the rule, not just extend it.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch whether Romy names the rule aloud before answering. If the child only guesses the next term without stating '+3' or '×2', slow down on find_unknown rounds.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will read and write simple fractions, match a fraction to its picture, and explain numerator and denominator in the child's own words.
lessons.rationale: This is the first lesson of the fractions block, so it starts at the very foundation: what the top and bottom numbers mean. No calculation yet, only meaning and recognition.
lessons.prerequisite: Equal sharing / halving a set into equal groups
lessons.mechanic_rationale: match_pairs and multi_choice tie the symbol to its picture; true_false and corridor_choice catch the classic 'bigger denominator = bigger fraction' misconception early.
lessons.assessment_focus: Check the child reads 3/4 as 'three quarters', not 'three four'. Note if the child confuses which number is on top — the core error to fix before lesson 3.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will identify and create halves, thirds and quarters by splitting shapes and sets into equal parts.
lessons.rationale: Having met the fraction symbol, Romy now works the three most common unit fractions concretely. Crystal Island's geometry mood suits dividing shapes into equal parts and checking the parts really are equal.
lessons.prerequisite: Knowing what numerator and denominator mean (lesson 2)
lessons.mechanic_rationale: balance_scale reinforces that all parts of a whole must be equal; drag_sort and match_pairs let the child group shapes by how many equal parts they show.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch for the child accepting unequal parts as 'a half'. The key sign of mastery is rejecting a shape split into two unequal pieces.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will shade a given fraction of a shape, name the fraction shaded, and spot an incorrectly labelled fraction.
lessons.rationale: This deepens lesson 3 from unit fractions to any fraction of a shape (e.g. 3/8), and adds the reverse skill — naming what is already shaded. Snowy Peaks' step-by-step mood fits the careful counting of shaded vs total parts.
lessons.prerequisite: Halves, thirds and quarters as equal parts (lesson 3)
lessons.mechanic_rationale: corridor_choice and true_false train the child to judge whether a label matches the picture; build_number lets the child assemble the fraction from shaded and total parts.
lessons.assessment_focus: Does the child count the total parts, not just the shaded ones? A common slip is writing 3/5 when 3 of 8 are shaded — flag it on the naming rounds.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will find a fraction of a set of objects (e.g. 1/4 of 12) and match sets to the fraction they show.
lessons.rationale: Fractions now jump from shapes to quantities — the bridge toward 'fraction of a number'. Returning to Jelly Bay (sharing/groups) links this back to the equal-sharing idea the child met in lesson 2, in a harder form.
lessons.prerequisite: Naming fractions of shapes (lesson 4) and knowing 2×/5×/10× facts
key_concepts: dividing a group into equal parts
lessons.mechanic_rationale: drag_group physically shares objects into equal parts; five_tasks builds fluency once the child sees that 'fraction of a set' is really divide-then-multiply.
lessons.assessment_focus: Check the child divides by the denominator first. If the child multiplies by the whole or guesses, revisit 'share into equal groups' before word problems.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will compare two simple fractions using >, < and =, and order a small set of fractions from smallest to largest.
lessons.rationale: This closes the fractions block and targets the trickiest fraction skill: judging fraction size. Antigravity Island's comparison/relational mood fits the >, <, = work and the counter-intuitive 'bigger bottom, smaller piece' rule.
lessons.mechanic_rationale: corridor_choice ('which side is bigger?') and drag_sort (order them) are the exact skills; balance_scale shows equivalent fractions balancing.
lessons.assessment_focus: The mastery sign is correctly saying 1/3 > 1/4. Watch for the reversed rule — comparing only the denominators as if bigger means more.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will read analogue clocks to the quarter hour, match digital to analogue times, and fill in missing times on a schedule.
lessons.rationale: A fresh topic and a new strand. Difficulty eases slightly to 5 for the new start. Neon City's clock/tech feel makes reading the hands feel like decoding a display.
lessons.prerequisite: Skip-counting in 5s (from lesson 1) for the minute marks
lessons.mechanic_rationale: match_pairs (digital↔analogue) and multi_choice train reading; number_grid lets the child hunt the cell with the matching time, keeping it playful.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch the classic swap of hour and minute hands, and 'quarter to' read as 'quarter past'. Note whether the child counts minutes in 5s reliably.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will calculate elapsed time, answer before/after questions, and read days and dates on a calendar.
lessons.rationale: Builds straight on lesson 7 from reading a clock to reasoning across time. Snowy Peaks' step-by-step logic suits counting on from a start time and working through a weekly planner.
lessons.prerequisite: Reading analogue and digital time to the quarter hour (lesson 7)
key_concepts: counting on in minutes and hours
days, weeks and dates on a calendar
lessons.mechanic_rationale: text_task frames real 'how long until…' problems; find_unknown asks for the missing start or end time; symbol_calc handles durations added together.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch how the child crosses the hour (e.g. 10:50 + 20 min). Counting minutes past 60 as if past 100 is the error to catch.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will count mixed coins and notes, make a given amount in more than one way, and compare prices.
lessons.rationale: A new, highly practical strand. Blue Crab Island's real-world/market mood is the natural home for money. Difficulty holds at 5 for the fresh start before shopping builds on it.
lessons.mechanic_rationale: build_number literally builds a total from coins and notes; drag_sort orders amounts and corridor_choice compares two prices.
lessons.assessment_focus: Check the child adds different coin values in a sensible order (largest first). Note if the child treats every coin as worth 1.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will calculate a shopping total, work out the correct change from a payment, and choose the best-value buy.
lessons.rationale: Applies lesson 9 to real transactions — the 'so what' of money. Mushroom Island's word-problem/growth mood fits a shopping story with totals and change.
lessons.mechanic_rationale: text_task carries the shopping story; find_unknown models change (paid − cost); key_lock frames 'unlock the till' as matching amounts to a target.
lessons.assessment_focus: Change is where subtraction across a 'ten' bites. Watch 20 − 13 style change and whether the child counts up or subtracts.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will choose the correct unit, estimate and compare measurements, and read a simple scale.
lessons.rationale: Opens the measurement-and-shape block. Returning to Blue Crab (real-world) keeps measuring grounded in objects the child can picture. Difficulty holds at 6, consolidating before perimeter's calculation.
lessons.mechanic_rationale: multi_choice and match_pairs link objects to sensible units; drag_sort orders lengths/weights and corridor_choice compares two measurements.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch unit sense — measuring a pencil in metres or a door in cm signals the child is not picturing the size. Note estimation vs exact confusion.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will calculate the perimeter of simple shapes, find a missing side length, and draw a shape to a given perimeter.
lessons.rationale: First true measurement calculation, building on lesson 11's length work. Antigravity Island's unknowns/equations mood matches the 'find the missing side' reverse task at the heart of this lesson.
key_concepts: perimeter of rectangles and irregular shapes
lessons.mechanic_rationale: find_unknown and balance_scale both model 'total perimeter fixed, one side missing'; symbol_calc handles perimeter formulas with repeated sides.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch whether the child adds every side (not just two) and how the child handles a missing side by subtracting from the total. Flag counting a side twice.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will name 2D and 3D shapes, count sides, vertices and faces, sort shapes, and identify right/acute/obtuse angles.
lessons.rationale: Completes the geometry strand begun with perimeter. Crystal Island's structure/geometry mood is the perfect home for classifying shapes and angles.
lessons.prerequisite: Familiarity with shapes and their sides (lesson 12)
lessons.mechanic_rationale: drag_group sorts 2D from 3D; match_pairs links shapes to their properties; corridor_choice and true_false test angle identification.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch the 2D/3D mix-up (square vs cube, circle vs sphere) and whether the child counts faces of a 3D shape rather than sides.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will read bar charts and pictograms, answer questions from data, and interpret a simple table.
lessons.rationale: Introduces handling data, drawing together counting, comparison and reading skills from across the plan. Neon City's display/tech mood fits reading charts like a dashboard.
lessons.mechanic_rationale: number_grid and multi_choice mirror reading values off a chart; drag_sort orders categories by size; text_task poses 'how many more' questions.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch pictogram keys where one symbol = 2 or 5 — reading it as 1 each is the classic error. Note whether the child uses the scale on a bar chart.

lessons.learning_objective: By the end of this lesson Romy will apply fractions, time, money, measurement and reasoning together to solve mixed multi-step problems.
lessons.rationale: The synthesis finale. It spirals back through every strand of the plan in a harder, mixed form to confirm mastery and celebrate the child's progress. Mushroom Island's growth mood suits a triumphant final challenge.
lessons.mechanic_rationale: five_tasks and symbol_calc demand multi-step reasoning; text_task and find_unknown mix topics into real problems, exactly the synthesis this lesson needs.
lessons.assessment_focus: Watch which strand slows the child down — that flags the topic to revisit next. Confidence and independent multi-step working are the mastery signals.
